content:
# Luke Chapter 9 — Disagreement Log
**Arbiter:** SOPHIA | **In council with:** EMET, LOGOS, PNEUMA
**Source Manuscript:** Codex Sinaiticus (Aleph, 01), PDF pages 97-99

---

## Summary Statistics

- **Total verses:** 62
- **Verses with full three-agent agreement:** 19 (9:1, 9:4, 9:9, 9:14, 9:15, 9:16, 9:18, 9:21, 9:22, 9:23, 9:24, 9:26, 9:27, 9:28, 9:30, 9:31, 9:34, 9:36, 9:50)
- **Verses with notable disagreements:** 43
- **Decisions favoring EMET:** 3
- **Decisions favoring LOGOS:** 28
- **Decisions favoring PNEUMA:** 2
- **Decisions blending sources:** 5
- **2-to-1 majority decisions:** 17
- **SOPHIA independent arbitrations:** 5

---

## Detailed Disagreements

| Verse | Issue | EMET (Literal) | LOGOS (Formal) | PNEUMA (Dynamic) | SOPHIA Decision | Rationale |
|-------|-------|----------------|----------------|-------------------|-----------------|-----------|
| 9:2 | "the sick" rendering | "the [ones] being sick" | "the sick" | "the sick" | "the sick" | 2-to-1; natural English; substantive meaning preserved |
| 9:3 | "silver" vs. "money" | "silver" | "money" | "money" | "money" | 2-to-1; functional meaning of *argyrion* in context |
| 9:5 | Conditional form | "as many as might not receive" | "as many as do not receive" | "If people do not welcome" | "as many as do not receive" | LOGOS balances fidelity with clarity; PNEUMA too free |
| 9:6 | Verb for evangelizing | "proclaiming-good-news" | "proclaiming the good news" | "announcing the good news" | "proclaiming the good news" | Natural rendering of *euangelizomenoi*; no hyphenation needed |
| 9:7 | Degree of perplexity | "he was perplexed" | "he was greatly perplexed" | "he was deeply troubled" | "he was greatly perplexed" | LOGOS adds "greatly" to capture imperfect *diēporei*; PNEUMA's "troubled" shifts meaning |
| 9:8 | "ancients" vs. "ancient ones" | "ancient [ones]" | "ancients" | "ancient prophets" | "ancients" | LOGOS is concise; PNEUMA adds "prophets" not in the Greek |
| 9:10 | "reported" vs. "recounted" | "recounted" | "reported" | "reported" | "reported" | 2-to-1; "reported" suits *diēgēsanto* |
| 9:12 | "lodge" vs. "find lodging" | "lodge and find provisions" | "find lodging and provisions" | "find food and lodging" | "find lodging and provisions" | LOGOS phrasing is most natural English |
| 9:17 | Leftover description | "twelve baskets of fragments" | "twelve baskets" of "broken pieces" | "twelve baskets of leftover pieces" | "twelve baskets" of broken pieces | LOGOS's "broken pieces" is precise for *klasmaton* |
| 9:20 | Question form | "who do you say me to be?" | "who do you say that I am?" | "Who do you say I am?" | "who do you say that I am?" | LOGOS preserves formality; EMET is overly literal word order |
| 9:25 | "destroyed" vs. "lost" | "having destroyed" | "having lost" | "lose" | "having lost" | *apolesas* can mean both; "lost" pairs naturally with "forfeited" |
| 9:29 | Garment description | "white, flashing like lightning" | "white, flashing like lightning" | "dazzling white, blazing like lightning" | "white, flashing like lightning" | 2-to-1; PNEUMA adds words not in source |
| 9:32 | Sleep description | "were having been weighed down with sleep" | "were weighed down with sleep" | "were weighed down with sleep" | "were weighed down with sleep" | 2-to-1; EMET's periphrastic is awkward in English |
| 9:33 | "tabernacles" vs. "tents" vs. "shelters" | "tents" | "tabernacles" | "shelters" | "tabernacles" | Preserves liturgical/typological resonance of *skēnas* in the Transfiguration |
| 9:35 | "hear" vs. "listen to" | "hear him" | "listen to him" | "Listen to him!" | "listen to him" | "Listen to" conveys obedient attention of *akouete* better |
| 9:38 | *monogenēs* rendering | "only-born" | "only-begotten" | "only child" | "only child" | Consistent with 8:42 SOPHIA decision; narrative context |
| 9:39 | "crushing" vs. "bruising" vs. "mauls" | "crushing him" | "bruising him" | "It mauls him" | "bruising him" | LOGOS's "bruising" for *syntribon* is precise; PNEUMA's "mauls" is too free |
| 9:41 | "faithless and perverted/perverse" | "faithless and having been perverted" | "faithless and perverse" | "unbelieving and perverse" | "faithless and perverse" | LOGOS is established biblical phrasing; EMET is awkward |
| 9:42 | "convulsed" detail | "convulsed [him]" | "convulsed him violently" | "threw him to the ground in a convulsion" | "convulsed him" | LOGOS adds "violently" not explicit in Greek; SOPHIA follows EMET's restraint with LOGOS's syntax |
| 9:44 | Ear idiom rendering | "place into your ears" | "Let these words sink into your ears" | "Let these words sink deep into your ears" | "Let these words sink into your ears" | LOGOS captures Semitic idiom naturally; EMET is over-literal; PNEUMA adds "deep" |
| 9:46 | *dialogismos* rendering | "a reasoning entered among them" | "an argument arose among them" | "An argument arose among the disciples" | "an argument arose among them" | LOGOS captures the disputative sense; PNEUMA adds "disciples" not in Greek |
| 9:47 | "little child" vs. "child" | "little child" | "child" | "little child" | "child" | Diminutive is inherent in *paidion* |
| 9:48 | "receives" vs. "welcomes" | "receives" | "receives" | "welcomes" | "receives" | 2-to-1; *dechomai* = "receive"; "welcome" is interpretive |
| 9:49 | Exorcist description word order | "someone in your name casting out" | "someone casting out demons in your name" | "someone driving out demons in your name" | "someone casting out demons in your name" | LOGOS word order is clearer English |
| 9:51 | *analēmpseōs* rendering | "being-taken-up" | "his taking up" | "for him to be taken up" | "his taking up" | LOGOS is a clean nominal form faithful to the Greek noun |
| 9:53 | Direction toward Jerusalem | "his face was going into Jerusalem" | "his face was set to go to Jerusalem" | "he was heading toward Jerusalem" | "his face was set toward Jerusalem" | Blends LOGOS's "set" (echoing v.51) with directional clarity |
| 9:54 | "tell" vs. "command" fire | "tell fire to come down" | "command fire to come down" | "call fire down" | "command fire to come down" | LOGOS's "command" better renders the authority implied |
| 9:58 | "birds of heaven" vs. "sky" | "birds of the heaven" | "birds of heaven" | "birds of the sky" | "birds of heaven" | 2-to-1; consistent with prior SOPHIA renderings of *tou ouranou* |
| 9:59 | "permit me" phrasing | "permit me, having gone away first" | "permit me first to go" | "first let me go" | "permit me first to go" | LOGOS is clear and faithful; PNEUMA slightly informal |
| 9:60 | "go and proclaim" form | "having gone away, proclaim" | "go and proclaim" | "go and proclaim" | "go and proclaim" | 2-to-1; more natural English |
| 9:61 | "say farewell" vs. "bid farewell" | "say farewell" | "bid farewell" | "say goodbye" | "bid farewell" | LOGOS's "bid farewell" is traditional; PNEUMA too informal |
| 9:62 | "plow" vs. "plough" spelling | "plow" | "plough" | "plow" | "plow" | 2-to-1; American English standard |
| 9:62 | "looking to the things behind" vs. "looks back" | "looking to the [things] behind" | "looks back" | "looks back" | "looks back" | 2-to-1; clear and natural |

---

## Key Textual Variants (Sinaiticus vs. Later Manuscripts)

These are not inter-agent disagreements but significant text-critical points where Sinaiticus diverges from the Byzantine/majority tradition, and where all three agents correctly followed the Sinaiticus reading:

| Verse | Sinaiticus Reading | Byzantine/Majority Reading | Significance |
|-------|-------------------|---------------------------|--------------|
| 9:3 | "neither staff" (ΜΗΤΕ ΡΑΒΔΟΝ) | Some traditions permit a staff | Synoptic divergence with Mark 6:8 preserved |
| 9:23 | "daily" (ΚΑΘ ΗΜΕΡΑΝ) included | Also present in most witnesses | Distinctly Lukan addition to cross-bearing saying |
| 9:28 | "about eight days" | Matthew/Mark: "after six days" | Different counting convention (inclusive vs. exclusive) |
| 9:29 | "different" (ΕΤΕΡΟΝ) | Matthew/Mark: "was transfigured" | Luke avoids *metamorphoō* terminology |
| 9:35 | "the Chosen One" (Ο ΕΚΛΕΛΕΓΜΕΝΟΣ) | "the Beloved" (Ο ΑΓΑΠΗΤΟΣ) | Major variant; Sinaiticus echoes Isaiah 42:1 |
| 9:54 | Shorter text (no "as Elijah did") | Some add Elijah reference | Later scribal expansion absent from Sinaiticus |
| 9:55-56 | Shorter text (rebuke only) | Some add extended speech about saving lives | Later scribal expansion absent from Sinaiticus |

---

*Compiled by SOPHIA in council with EMET, LOGOS, and PNEUMA, from Codex Sinaiticus (4th century CE).*
